The occasion soon turns sour when the island is shaken by a torrential storm, and ranch owner Hannah advises the men to wait it out in their tents. By morning, Jack thinks he has seen the worst of it, until he opens up the locked police station and finds the body of a man lying in the middle of the floor. Its Adam, with scratches on his arms and laceration marks around his neck

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Radio quiz Up to You comes to Kembleford and the sleuth suspects one of the cast is responsible when the landlady of the house they are staying at is murdered. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: In the Footsteps of a Killer
programme.desc: Another mystery for the cops on the island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. However, its all change for the team. DI Jack Mooney (Ardal OHanlon) is now in charge, and his first Caribbean case sees the reinvestigation of a seven-year-old murder when a tourist provides an alibi for the woman convicted of the crime

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a motel room. The door is found to have been chain-locked from the inside, so Alexa is left to ponder how the killer managed to escape. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Inspector Brackenreid learns that his youngest son, Bobby, is wanted for murder, while Murdoch and Crabtree suspect old enemies are returning to harm them

programme.title: The Diplomat
programme.desc: A British child is abducted, requiring Lauras diplomatic skills as she tries to support both parents during a media storm. Meanwhile, the Sutherland case takes an alarming turn

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: After the electrocuted body of a man is found, Murdoch and Brackenreid investigate a pair of researchers known as The Frankensteins for their bizarre psychological studies

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch and Ogdens weekend at a country inn goes awry when a guest is murdered

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of a Colonial Postal Service officer when the fair comes to Kembleford. Drama, starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death and her body discovered in a forest, and suspicion falls on her missing friend, who has seemingly fled the scene. However, when the suspect resurfaces in a visibly shaken state, and with no memory of the crucial nights events, Jane is forced to consider the involvement of a third party. Simon Baker stars

programme.title: Hudson and Rex
programme.desc: With the rest of the team racing to stop an illicit weapons sale, Rex resolves to bring justice all on his own when he witnesses a murder while stuck at home

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Alexa is tasked to infiltrate the world of lycra when Hugh Miller, the chief financial officer of a huge hospitality company, dies mysteriously while on his morning bicycle ride. She quickly identifies a prime suspect in Roger Sims, the CEO of the hospitality business. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can DI Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal OHanlon stars

programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests Dara O Briain, Denise Van Outen, Rhod Gilbert and Vernon Kay, who try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return with another episode of memorable moments from the hit comedy panel show. programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britains fondness for picnics, preparing an alfresco lunch at Beaufort Polo Club in the Cotswolds. programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.title: Railroad Australia

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Former Soccer Saturday colleagues Jeff Stelling and Chris Kamara reunite for an epic journey across Sri Lanka where they reaffirm their friendship along the way

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads

Actor Eric Bana is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May try to impress Oxford residents by building an electric car, and refuse to let an unenthusiastic response deter them from sitting in on the machines crash-test before handing it over to the Stigs environmentally friendly cousin. Actor Michael Sheen is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1 against the Audi R8 V10

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes

programme.title: QI XL

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: Hate Thy Neighbour
programme.desc: Jamali Maddix spends time with two passionate but very different pro-life preachers during a period where the abortion debate has been reignited

programme.title: Dalziel and Pascoe

David Soul, Phyllis Logan and Lee Boardman guest star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharons husband Chris becomes seriously ill, leaving her facing a difficult decision. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph star in an episode featuring an early screen appearance by Doon Mackichan

programme.title: Copper
programme.sub-title: The Hope Too Bright to Last

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lennox is furious when a suspected robber accuses him of racism

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel saves the surgery when an angry patient threatens to drive a truck into the building after discovering his wifes affair. Ronnie moves back home, but puts his things into the spare room, and Jimmi sees Eva for the first time since she left him. With guest star Heather Peace

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Michelle visits a reclusive spinster and discovers the woman has been hiding a son with a facial deformity. She also learns the man thought to be the boys uncle was actually his father and did not die of cancer as originally thought, but committed suicide. Eva questions Maisie about the attack in the park

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Although pleased to be offered a new job at the hospital, Kath begins to question her life and decides to leave Holby altogether to spend more time with Larry - but before going, she finally admits to Lisa that she did help Terry die. Theres also drama over at Mubbss flat as Rosies daughters annoy him so much he hits one of them, while Ric turns up at work with a cut on his head, and George gets another new heart thanks to Eds latest underhand tactics

programme.title: Casualty

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small

programme.title: Howards Way

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Barry in Danger from Reading and Aunt Jessie

programme.sub-title: Whos That Merry Man with Billy, Then?

programme.desc: Standing loses £10,000 in a high-stakes poker game - but his opponent, a bookmaker, agrees to write off the debt if the detective can bring his fathers killer to justice. Meanwhile, a widow mounts a protest outside the station

The investigation takes a surprising turn when a second body is found on the beach. Ricky Tomlinson and John McArdle guest star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

programme.sub-title: Hal Cruttenden, Justin Moorhouse, Tom Stade
programme.desc: Hal Cruttenden hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, warming up the audience before comedy circuit regulars Justin Moorhouse and Tom Stade take the mic

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: The boys join Mrs Linekers Love Bureau hoping to find their perfect love matches. Richies video tells the potential suitors that he is, in fact, a duke and this secures him a date with a countess from Moldavia. Undeterred, he invites her to his place and arranges for Eddie to pretend to be his butler for the evening. Chaotic comedy, written by and starring Adrian Edmondson and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: Desperate duo Richie and Eddie try to improve their sex lives by using a spray apparently able to make them attractive to the opposite sex. Starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary agrees to see a relationship counsellor, while Tonys efforts to impress Deborahs mother end in disaster. To compound their woes, the duo fear the arrival of Dorothys 10-year-old nephew will ruin their efforts to act maturely. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin

programme.desc: The boys join Mrs Linekers Love Bureau hoping to find their perfect love matches. Richies video tells the potential suitors that he is, in fact, a duke and this secures him a date with a countess from Moldavia. Undeterred, he invites her to his place and arranges for Eddie to pretend to be his butler for the evening. Chaotic comedy, written by and starring Adrian Edmondson and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: Desperate duo Richie and Eddie try to improve their sex lives by using a spray apparently able to make them attractive to the opposite sex. Starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary agrees to see a relationship counsellor, while Tonys efforts to impress Deborahs mother end in disaster. To compound their woes, the duo fear the arrival of Dorothys 10-year-old nephew will ruin their efforts to act maturely. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Hal Cruttenden, Justin Moorhouse, Tom Stade
programme.desc: Hal Cruttenden hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, warming up the audience before comedy circuit regulars Justin Moorhouse and Tom Stade take the mic

programme.title: Nurses Down Under
programme.desc: In Emergency, nurse Carly treats the broken bones of the weekends sporting warriors

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Lagos
programme.desc: The journalist meets gangs known as Area Boys as they patrol their neighbourhoods in the Nigerian city of Lagos, clashing with rivals in a bid to keep the peace on the streets. He is taken under the wing of their leader MC, a street youth who is now friends with the most powerful men in the city, and also spends time with the governments paramilitary tasks force, whose ruthless tactics are not that different to those of the gangs

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some peoples most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Back from the Dead: Who Kidnapped Me?
programme.desc: A woman spots a young girl she believes is the baby she lost in a house fire six years earlier. The encounter triggers an investigation and an astonishing discovery

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: The Top 5 hit the road for a three-day surprise adventure in regional Victoria. First stop is the stunning All Saints Estate for a classic MasterChef time auction

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel Britain searching for items to fix up and flog for their owners. In East Sussex and Hertfordshire, the duos finds include a Victorian egg incubator

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS Children in Need Special 2023
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his team of volunteer trades and suppliers head to Treetops Hospice in Risley, Derbyshire, for an ambitious Big Build to support and help more children and families experiencing traumatic loss. Radio 2 presenters Scott Mills and Zoe Ball and musician Sam Ryder also don their safety boots, hi-vis and hard hats to get stuck in, while Richie Anderson and Owain Wyn Evans meet some of the young people who have lost loved ones and will benefit from the new building

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Essex, where local mental health charity Butterfly Effect Well-being is in desperate need of a new meeting place. They have an army of local volunteer trades - and the cast of EastEnders, who have made the trip from Walford to help transform a derelict building into a warm and welcoming space for the local community

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A couple bring their 13-month-old cocker spaniel puppy in for scheduled surgery for hip dysplasia which will require two separate full hip replacements. Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel diagnoses the joint condition osteochondritis dissecans in a hyperactive ridgeback named Loki, and discusses treatment options with his owners. Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing stars Anton Du Beke, Katya Jones and Graziano Di Prima join Nick Knowles and the team in Wallsend, Newcastle. Together, they set to work transforming the True Colours performing arts school, an inclusive dance school that teaches dance and theatre skills to 500 children and young adults weekly, many of whom have disabilities and learning needs. The school has grown substantially over time and theyve completely outgrown their existing space, so the team get to work renovating a run-down old boys club that would give the dance school the extra space it so desperately needs to continue to transform more young lives

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS Children in Need Special 2023
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his team of volunteer trades and suppliers head to Treetops Hospice in Risley, Derbyshire, for an ambitious Big Build to support and help more children and families experiencing traumatic loss. Radio 2 presenters Scott Mills and Zoe Ball and musician Sam Ryder also don their safety boots, hi-vis and hard hats to get stuck in, while Richie Anderson and Owain Wyn Evans meet some of the young people who have lost loved ones and will benefit from the new building

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A woman fights for her life after being knifed by her former partner, while a teenager on her way to seeing two YouTube stars in a live appearance is felled by a falling tree

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Three more expectant mothers visit Leeds maternity wards. Katie, 21, and Jonathan, 22, were not together when she found out she was pregnant, but they were reunited 12 weeks later at the first scan, and at that moment Jonathan realised he wanted to be a good father. Meanwhile, 20-year-old Nicole is admitted with a significant bleed, and the midwives race to fill the bath for 30-year-old Jolanta, who has experienced quick labours in the past and wants a water birth for her third baby

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The documentary follows two expectant mothers whose unplanned pregnancies happened at very different ages. Eighteen-year-old Laura, who has developed gestational diabetes, is booked in for an early induction, but shes rushed to theatre when her babys heartbeat drops. programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An energetic one-year-old bull terrier puppy called Apple comes into the surgery with an ankle problem, and Noel hopes a custom-made, 3D-printed implant may give this beloved pet a new lease of life. He also treats a two-year-old Siamese cat with a broken leg, shattered pelvis and suspected spinal injury after being hit by a van, while four-year-old collie cross Rosco is tested for hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A six-month-old collie appears to have a developmental deformity where the bones in its elbows dont fit together, but after CT scans, Noel sees that the situation is even more complex. A Burmese mountain dog has a painful bulging disc, which Noel hopes to replace with a bespoke titanium spacer screw, while cat Elmo has a complex fracture to its left back leg after being hit by a car

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team enlist the help of a local community in Aberystwyth, Ceredigion, to renovate the home of a builder with a brain tumour. They finish the construction of an extension to the mans home, which had been left unfinished owing to his ill health, and call on the skills of his friends and family to complete the job

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team enlist the help of the local community as they set out to renovate a property in Durham. The DIY experts work on the house of a family with a disabled teenager, whose condition has kept them apart for a year because the home was not equipped to suit his needs

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Paramedic Kim climbs into a crushed car balanced over a fast-flowing river, while services across the Pennines are paralysed when a man plunges from a bridge onto a busy rail line

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Behind Bars
programme.desc: The journalist goes inside Americas notorious, overcrowded San Quentin State Prison and meets some of its 3,000 inmates, including a man serving a 521-year sentence and a transgender couple who live like husband and wife. He also learns how the convicts have created their own world, complete with a brutal code of conduct, and how they cope with the prospect of spending the rest of their days behind bars

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In 2007, when Paris Bennett was 13 years old, stabbed his four-year-old sister to death, a crime for which he has been behind bars ever since. Now 25 and in his first interview on British television, he tells Piers Morgan how this was no impulsive fit of rage but rather a cold, deliberate act planned as an act of revenge against his mother Charity - who is watching the interview unfold in a nearby room. She has stood by him even though she fears that when he gets out, he could still harbour violent intentions towards her - or even worse, towards his younger brother

programme.title: Nurses Down Under
programme.desc: A roofer falls three metres from a ladder, while a 52-year-old man with end-stage heart failure might not get his life-saving heart transplant

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Johannesburg
programme.desc: Award-winning journalist Louis Theroux visits Johannesburg, where locals have turned to an industry of private security firms after losing trust in the police

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Back from the Dead: Who Kidnapped Me?
programme.desc: Delimars search for answers leads to an emotional reunion, new revelations and a confrontation with her father

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Lagos
programme.desc: The journalist meets gangs known as Area Boys as they patrol their neighbourhoods in the Nigerian city of Lagos, clashing with rivals in a bid to keep the peace on the streets. He is taken under the wing of their leader MC, a street youth who is now friends with the most powerful men in the city, and also spends time with the governments paramilitary tasks force, whose ruthless tactics are not that different to those of the gangs

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some peoples most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Back from the Dead: Who Kidnapped Me?
programme.desc: A woman spots a young girl she believes is the baby she lost in a house fire six years earlier. The encounter triggers an investigation and an astonishing discovery

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS Children in Need Special 2023
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his team of volunteer trades and suppliers head to Treetops Hospice in Risley, Derbyshire, for an ambitious Big Build to support and help more children and families experiencing traumatic loss. Radio 2 presenters Scott Mills and Zoe Ball and musician Sam Ryder also don their safety boots, hi-vis and hard hats to get stuck in, while Richie Anderson and Owain Wyn Evans meet some of the young people who have lost loved ones and will benefit from the new building

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Essex, where local mental health charity Butterfly Effect Well-being is in desperate need of a new meeting place. They have an army of local volunteer trades - and the cast of EastEnders, who have made the trip from Walford to help transform a derelict building into a warm and welcoming space for the local community

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A couple bring their 13-month-old cocker spaniel puppy in for scheduled surgery for hip dysplasia which will require two separate full hip replacements. Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel diagnoses the joint condition osteochondritis dissecans in a hyperactive ridgeback named Loki, and discusses treatment options with his owners. Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing stars Anton Du Beke, Katya Jones and Graziano Di Prima join Nick Knowles and the team in Wallsend, Newcastle. Together, they set to work transforming the True Colours performing arts school, an inclusive dance school that teaches dance and theatre skills to 500 children and young adults weekly, many of whom have disabilities and learning needs. The school has grown substantially over time and theyve completely outgrown their existing space, so the team get to work renovating a run-down old boys club that would give the dance school the extra space it so desperately needs to continue to transform more young lives

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS Children in Need Special 2023

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A woman fights for her life after being knifed by her former partner, while a teenager on her way to seeing two YouTube stars in a live appearance is felled by a falling tree

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Three more expectant mothers visit Leeds maternity wards. Katie, 21, and Jonathan, 22, were not together when she found out she was pregnant, but they were reunited 12 weeks later at the first scan, and at that moment Jonathan realised he wanted to be a good father. Meanwhile, 20-year-old Nicole is admitted with a significant bleed, and the midwives race to fill the bath for 30-year-old Jolanta, who has experienced quick labours in the past and wants a water birth for her third baby

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave is off to London where a couple of very special Fords, including a pristine Escort Cosworth, are hidden away in a garage

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle thats ripe for restoration

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Up for auction this week is a 1980 Ford Escort, a hand-built 1946 Bedford 12 electric car, and a beautiful, red 1957 Austin Healey.

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: The historian investigates the complex factors that shaped the dark imagination of a refined Devonshire lady, discovering family secrets and a childhood haunted by a sinister figure. Focusing on the first third of Agatha Christies life, this episode unearths the surprising roots of her most compelling themes, the inspiration for some of her greatest creations, and the secrets that the enigmatic Christie herself kept carefully hidden from public view

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the Severn to begin a trip along the south coast of Wales, joining maintenance workers on a hair-raising secret railway high above the estuary. From Newport, Michael makes for Caerleon, where he discovers how its Roman history was eclipsed by the legend of King Arthur and his Round Table

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his south Wales coastal railway journey in Barry Island, where he joins a Bristol pilot on his way to help guide a feeder container ship into port. Skirting the Glamorgan Heritage Coast, Michael alights at Llantwit Major to visit St Donats Castle, before reaching the pretty seaside resort of Porthcawl

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Port Talbot to Pembrey Burry Port
programme.desc: Michael Portillos coastal rail journey along the south coast of Wales reaches the town of Port Talbot, where he investigates how the steel industry is planning to decarbonise. Across the Bay, Michael arrives in The Mumbles, site of the worlds first passenger railway in 1807, before making his way from Gowerton to Burry Port and heading for the wild and blustery Pembrey Sands

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the rural coastline of Carmarthenshire and Pembrokeshire in south Wales. From Carmarthen Bay, he tracks up the estuary and River Towy to the historic town of Carmarthen and Middleton Hall, home of Waless National Botanic Garden. There, Michael is on the trail of the quarter of a million honeybees that live in the garden

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a beautifully restored and carefully modified 1936 Wolseley 14/56. Meanwhile, a Batman-inspired Chrysler Crossfire is dropped off at the Thornton le Dale garage

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls doing the pick-ups in Lancashire where a rare 1960 Vauxhall Cresta PA Friary estate in dusky pink awaits

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Lancaster factory, one of the biggest buildings in Europe at the time, helped to create a truly war-winning weapon

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Partys militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Concorde Hangar at the Aerospace Bristol museum, where the most surprising item brought in is a very valuable statue of Buddha. Other finds being valued include an intricate model of a vintage car made by a prisoner of war, an Elizabethan ring, a collection of classic guitars, and of course, a great variety of Concorde memorabilia

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks grandchildren, Jack and Amy, head to the North East of England to pick up some charming old bikes. Derek helps a collector downsize and discovers a rare Chrysler Alpine

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930s Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Lancaster factory, one of the biggest buildings in Europe at the time, helped to create a truly war-winning weapon

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the worlds tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the states changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. Ten days later, Agatha was discovered in a hotel in Harrogate, claiming to have lost her memory. Lucy Worsley delves into this traumatic, watershed moment in Christies life. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the most westerly station in Wales, on the final leg of his rail journey along its southern coastline. At Milford Haven, Michael learns from the Harbour Master about the biggest energy port in the United Kingdom, which has an oil refinery and storage terminals as well as natural gas terminals. Further along the estuary at Pembroke Dock, Michael visits a pioneering project to harness the power of the sea

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo returns to travel by train to more places around the British Isles, beginning by heading to the self-governing Isle of Man - a paradise for rail lovers. His first stop on the Manx Electric Railway is the capital of Douglas, where he reveals the islands parliament the Tynwald is older than Westminster, before the Isle of Man Steam Railway takes him down the east coast to Castletown, from where he heads to the Norse Castle Rusheword

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey at Liverpools historic waterfront, where he visits the International Slavery Museum at Royal Albert Dock, before heading underground to discover one of the most important and secret rooms of the Second World War, the Royal Navys Western Approaches Headquarters. His next stop is Crosby on the Merseyside coastline in Sefton, before making his way north to Blackpool, the last stop on this leg of his journey

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey across the vast and beautiful Morecambe Bay, where he joins one of the towns last shrimpers to find out how potted shrimp is made, From there, Michael makes his way to Arnside on the Furness Line, where he discovers a magnificently quirky 17th century garden, and across the Arnside viaduct, he reaches Cartmel and its 12th century priory and a Michelin-starred restaurant

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Focusing on the mass production of ammunition brought on by Samuel Colt, and the inadequacy of Soviet factory production which made the AK47 so effective

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Documentary examining the rule of Adolf Hitler and his party, focusing on the Eastern Front during the Second World War, which many see as the defining arena of the conflict. In the autumn of 1940, Adolf Hitler has to decide what to do about the Soviet Union and its leader Joseph Stalin. He offers to carve up the post-war world in exchange for his fellow dictators support in the war against Britain, but is actually preparing an attack on the USSR

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, one of the first stately homes in Britain to be converted into a hospital and convalescent home during the First World War. Items brought in to be valued include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to North Wales to collect an Austin 7, and a gem of a Bentley, a British classic with coachwork built by top notch Parisian outfit Kellner who specialised in luxury

Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the countrys most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A low-mileage Renault Dauphine proves irresistible to the team, but its 1959 mechanics ensure some hefty bills

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: In 1930, recovering from a personal crisis, Agatha Christie fell in love with and married Max Mallowan. This relationship, and the security it brought, saw Agatha enter the most prolific and successful chapter of her career. This would be the period in which Agatha penned some of her most famous and enduring bestsellers, including Murder on the Orient Express. Lucy follows in Agathas footsteps to discover the roots of these works and explores how, in later life, Agatha achieved global celebrity - but remained very much the anonymous observer, hiding in plain sight

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle thats ripe for restoration

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Up for auction this week is a 1980 Ford Escort, a hand-built 1946 Bedford 12 electric car, and a beautiful, red 1957 Austin Healey.

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: The historian investigates the complex factors that shaped the dark imagination of a refined Devonshire lady, discovering family secrets and a childhood haunted by a sinister figure. Focusing on the first third of Agatha Christies life, this episode unearths the surprising roots of her most compelling themes, the inspiration for some of her greatest creations, and the secrets that the enigmatic Christie herself kept carefully hidden from public view

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the Severn to begin a trip along the south coast of Wales, joining maintenance workers on a hair-raising secret railway high above the estuary. From Newport, Michael makes for Caerleon, where he discovers how its Roman history was eclipsed by the legend of King Arthur and his Round Table

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his south Wales coastal railway journey in Barry Island, where he joins a Bristol pilot on his way to help guide a feeder container ship into port. Skirting the Glamorgan Heritage Coast, Michael alights at Llantwit Major to visit St Donats Castle, before reaching the pretty seaside resort of Porthcawl

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Port Talbot to Pembrey Burry Port
programme.desc: Michael Portillos coastal rail journey along the south coast of Wales reaches the town of Port Talbot, where he investigates how the steel industry is planning to decarbonise. Across the Bay, Michael arrives in The Mumbles, site of the worlds first passenger railway in 1807, before making his way from Gowerton to Burry Port and heading for the wild and blustery Pembrey Sands

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the rural coastline of Carmarthenshire and Pembrokeshire in south Wales. From Carmarthen Bay, he tracks up the estuary and River Towy to the historic town of Carmarthen and Middleton Hall, home of Waless National Botanic Garden. There, Michael is on the trail of the quarter of a million honeybees that live in the garden

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a beautifully restored and carefully modified 1936 Wolseley 14/56. Meanwhile, a Batman-inspired Chrysler Crossfire is dropped off at the Thornton le Dale garage

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls doing the pick-ups in Lancashire where a rare 1960 Vauxhall Cresta PA Friary estate in dusky pink awaits

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Lancaster factory, one of the biggest buildings in Europe at the time, helped to create a truly war-winning weapon

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Partys militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Concorde Hangar at the Aerospace Bristol museum, where the most surprising item brought in is a very valuable statue of Buddha. Other finds being valued include an intricate model of a vintage car made by a prisoner of war, an Elizabethan ring, a collection of classic guitars, and of course, a great variety of Concorde memorabilia

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Salvage Squad
programme.desc: Brian Dowling and his fashion-forward design team continue their mission to prove to the nation that it is possible achieve a dream home renovation at little cost. Changing Rooms meets the Repair Shop with a new sustainable twist, the challenge here is to transform our bland old rooms, furniture and fittings into unique and stunning show stoppers, using only upcycling and sustainable methods. This is the home makeover format for the world we live in today, as two households per week, each with a team consisting of an interior designer and an up-cycling wiz, have just four days and 1,000 euro to renovate and rejuvenate a tired spaces in their home, filling them with home-made, re-used and revamped furnishings. Two families are selected from the same region each week and each has a tired room in need of renovation and re-imagining containing a potential treasure trove of up-cycling gems

programme.title: The Royal
programme.sub-title: Cometh the Hour, Cometh the Man
programme.desc: Mr Middleditch brings his reign to an end and hands over the ledgers to the new administrator - having craftily secured the hospitals future. Dr Ormerods plaster comes off, Mr Rose risks his own skin to save a friend during a game of golf, and a trivial accident leads a family to make a major and heartbreaking discovery. Ian Carmichael, Robert Cavanah, Robert Daws and Wendy Craig star

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The stories of two sons who were given up for adoption and the families who have stopped at nothing to find them. Andrew Barlow was adopted as a baby, but his birth mother Patricia Clark refused to sign the adoption papers and never gave up looking for him, while as a child, Julie Redman discovered that her mother Joan had given up her older brother for adoption

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to win the affections of Dom, with the buyer of the clothing picked out by the former goth going on a date with him

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in the ultimate shopping challenge, as they try to win the affections of picky photographer Danielle by each buying her an outfit

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: The contestants are guaranteed some fine food today as their mystery host is celebrity chef Aldo Zilli. Can he cook up enough cryptic clues to hide his Italian identity or will he be getting out the Fairy Liquid when the diners have all gone home? Brian McFadden plays go-between

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as the remaining four famous faces compete in the semi-final as they take on healthy dishes. The two stars who fail to impress the judges will end up in the cook-off, with the pair fighting it out for the last place in the grand final

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Tristan Welch and Philli Armitage-Mattin at the Wimpole Estate in Cambridgeshire to see who can create and cook the Dish of the Day. Ainsley makes beetroot and carrot patties with a vegan horseradish yoghurt, Tristan brings his passion for zero-waste cooking to the kitchen with his honey and lemon roasted carrots with spiced-up beans, and Philli uses her knowledge of Japanese cuisine to make a summer vegetable pancake with garlic aioli

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2006

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories looking back at the headlines of the past, focusing here on 2007

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the foothills of the Himalayas to investigate stories of deadly attacks by the man-eating goonch catfish, tracking down the species to establish if its methods of killing are as gruesome as reports suggest

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: Baggage Manager Kevin has a rough day trouble-shooting a number of mechanical breakdowns, while security screening discovers a passenger travelling with a gun

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Labrador pup Bonnie must undergo life-saving heart surgery, while ragdoll cat owner John needs to uncover what is wrong with his favourite feline

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Vickie from Middlesex, Julie from Vale of Glamorgan, Angus from Newcastle and Tim from the Isle of Man answer general knowledge questions and work as a team to take on one of the Chasers and secure a cash prize. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

Changing Rooms meets the Repair Shop with a new sustainable twist, the challenge here is to transform our bland old rooms, furniture and fittings into unique and stunning show stoppers, using only upcycling and sustainable methods. This is the home makeover format for the world we live in today, as two households per week, each with a team consisting of an interior designer and an up-cycling wiz, have just four days and 1,000 euro to renovate and rejuvenate a tired spaces in their home, filling them with home-made, re-used and revamped furnishings. Two families are selected from the same region each week and each has a tired room in need of renovation and re-imagining containing a potential treasure trove of up-cycling gems

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A local nightclub owner is admitted with symptoms of poisoning, and his son is quick to point the finger of suspicion at his fathers glamorous wife. Frankie is shocked to see Dr Ormerod doubt himself as they tend a drunken farmer after a tractor accident, while Dr Goodwin deals with a case of domestic abuse and a stationery salesman takes a shine to Lizzie. Robert Daws and Scott Taylor star

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons attempt to impress cheerleader Lily Amelia with their choice of outfit from Leeds city centre - which of them will be successful and be chosen for a prized date?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress rugby player Jakob Williams in Cardiff, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes he liked best

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Boxing promoter Kellie Maloney throws a dinner party for four strangers at her Essex home. Brian McFadden hosts the game show where diners try to guess the identity of a mystery host holding a dinner party in order to win a cash prize

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the grand final of the celebrity cooking contest - and the pressure is on as the three remaining stars battle it out in their toughest challenge yet. They take on their mentors signature dishes and the two who make it through to the final round will have to deliver a three-course meal in an epic head-to-head battle in a bid to lift the Golden Frying Pan

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Lerato Umah-Shaylor and Freddy Forster in the kitchen garden of Blickling Hall in Norfolk to see who can create and cook the Dish Of The Day this time. Ainsley showcases cucumbers picked from the Blickling greenhouse as part of his chicken and cucumber noodle salad, Freddys background in French cuisine is shown in his sea trout with chilli and strawberry butter sauce, and Lerato brings her expertise in traditional African cooking to a Ugandan rolex

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past, focusing here on 2008

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2009

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: America boasts a species of fish reputed to have committed a number of violent attacks on humans. The alligator gar is said to be as vicious as a shark and as large as an alligator. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Texas on a hunt to uncover the truth about the notorious creature

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: Lightning has struck an incoming flight. As engineers assess the damage, high winds lead to runway closures and another plane leaks a potentially dangerous liquid

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Chinook, a fluffy Samoyed dog, is brought in to see if the tumour on his liver is operable. Mirabella the cat arrives for a radical radium treatment that only the vets can provide in a last-ditch effort to treat her thyroid issues. Ruas owner is concerned he may have epilepsy, while a bull comes to the hospital for a delicate operation

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Soo from Halifax, Dom from North Tyneside, Nichola from Bangor and Will from London pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Trigger Point
programme.desc: All hope looks lost for the remaining missing person, until a new lead emerges. With Lana now known to the bomber, she has to work harder than ever to avoid falling into his traps, pushing her to breaking point

programme.title: Living with Lucy
programme.desc: Presenter Lucy Kennedy moves in with comedian Deirdre OKane in her Dublin home, and gets the benefit of her expert interior decorating advice

programme.desc: Detective Holly ORourke and her homicide team as a seemingly open-and-shut murder investigation threatens to destroy her career, her family, and her faith in justice. Drama, starring Leanna Walsman, Stephen Peacocke and Matt Day

programme.title: Human Error
programme.desc: After learning the mystery hit-man shot the wrong woman, Holly and her team race to identify the real target before she is killed. Drama, starring Leanna Walsman, Stephen Peacocke and Matt Day

programme.title: Spies of Terror
programme.desc: After a terrorism attack in Paris, government agents race to catch those responsible. Spy thriller, starring Rachida Brakni, Fleur Geffrier, Vincent Elbaz and Pierre Perrier. In French

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of 84-year-old Diana Bramall, who struck up a close bond with her carer, Donna White. However, three years in, Diana noticed money missing from her handbag. Her son Stephen suggested installing a kitchen CCTV camera, and the footage proved shocking

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker manages to arrest one member of a robber gang when a sting operation goes wrong - but ends up being taken hostage by the mans brother. The PCs disappearance is bad news for Blaketon, whos frantic to pull together a winning side for the annual cricket match against rivals Scarsdale. Cricketers Martin Bicknell, Ray Illingworth and Brian Close guest star, alongside Dickie Bird as the umpire

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sam prepare to celebrate their first wedding anniversary. Cassie welcomes Grey House guest Joy, who seems to be spying on the Merriwicks. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of Londons Crossrail network, a project of staggering proportions, with 10,000 personnel working across 40 sites and on 118km of track. The focus should have been on the impressive engineering feat, though delays and budgets have become the focus of attention

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Yvonne Cobb and Rupy Aujla at Tyntesfield in Somerset to see who can create and cook the Dish of the Day this time. Ainsley collects greengages and plums for his brioche French toast, chargrilled plums and cinnamon creme fraiche, Yvonne adds delicious flavour hacks to a creamy tarragon chicken and French bean salad with roasted rosemary potatoes, and Rupy talks up the health benefits of his grilled mackerel, roasted apple, radish and chilli and pea pesto

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. A key witness turns the investigation on its head after revealing the body was found with a single puncture wound, and Jeremy conducts an experiment to prove his unlikely new suspect is capable of pulling an adult under the water

programme.title: Peter Andres 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter Andres 60 Minute Makeover is back and bigger than ever on Quest Red. Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home mkeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes. In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A feud between warring neighbours turns into a siege when Nick is held at gunpoint. Meanwhile, Kate puts her own life at risk to save a dying man. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry examines the impact that Spring has upon the natural world, a season which sees new life erupting across the Northern hemisphere as temperatures begin to rise. Further south in Kenya, a cheetah mother struggles to bring up her young in the wet season, and in Trinidad, turtle hatchlings begin their instinctive march towards the sea

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Madonna Halley Hotel in north London, run by members of a Cypriot family whose Mediterranean roots are reflected in the interior design. Guests have previously indicated the Aegean-style fixtures and fittings can be disagreeable, but when the presenter suggests a radical overhaul, the owners reveal they are reluctant to part with their distinctive adornments

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah sets Cain straight, while Liam urges Cain to inform Moira, who then struggles with the news

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo uses his birthday to make Todd pay, Jodie resents Adam for lecturing her, Leanne worries about Sam, and Lauren puts a dampener on Ollie and Amys date

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Nurse Stella Davenport arrives at the Royal with a less than favourable reputation, and soon causes a stir among the staff and patients as rumours about her circulate the hospital. Matron and Harper discover a new approach to interviewing when they try to fill a ward domestic vacancy, while Dr Ormerod is left to deal with the dramatic aftermath of a domestic dispute, and Lizzie realises her new scooter might have been an ill-advised purchase. Medical drama starring Natalie Anderson, Robert Daws and Wendy Craig

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick tackles a tidy burglar who insists on clearing up the houses he robs, and investigates a suspicious church organist. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is found buried on the Northumberland moors which could be a missing teenager from the nearby rural community of Brindale. The girl had disappeared six weeks previously, and Vera has to break the news to her devastated family. However, the realisation that she died so close to home rather than at a music festival as originally thought creates tension among her neighbours, who are soon suspecting each other of murder. The detective delves into the vulnerable young womans isolated existence to identify her killer

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary intervenes to reconcile Todd and Theo, Maggie orders Lauren to show Ollie what hes missing, Alya receives a concerning message, and Leanne overhears Megan flirting on the phone

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain needs to face up to what his future might hold, while Joe feels unappreciated and Kim is surprised when she pays a visit to Moira

programme.title: Essex County
programme.desc: A story of loss and loneliness tracing the interconnected lives of two families in a rural community. Canadian drama, based on the graphic novel by Jeff Lemire

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: The submarine is left exposed by a reactor shutdown, and the crew races to repair the fault. Kirsten uncovers a feud on board the Vigil, leading Amy to question whether her investigation has been deliberately obstructed. A hard-won breakthrough leads Amy to believe she finally has the killer in her sights, only for surprising new evidence to cast doubt on her theory. On land, Kirstens investigation leads to tragedy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police search for a boy who went missing after discovering his mother was having an affair. Younger strikes up an unlikely friendship as he fills in for the local lollipop lady, while Walker finds he isnt over Helen during a date with one of Ginas friends, and Carols fiance Frank gets time off from the Navy to visit her. Sixties-set drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie helps Joy mark her mothers birthday by planting a tree and discovers a time capsule that Grace buried years ago. Sam competes against Middleton favourites in a chili cook-off. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of the Tokyo Skytree, the worlds tallest free-standing tower, which uses liquid springs and safety glass to withstand earthquakes and high winds

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Daniel Galmiche and Ruby Bhogal at the Wimpole Estate in Cambridgeshire to see who can create and cook the Dish of the Day this time. Ainsley digs up some spuds for his quick flatbread pizza with Charlotte potatoes, rosemary and chilli rings, Daniel brings his French fervour to a pork chop with courgette, tomatoes and cavolo nero, and Ruby celebrates her Indian heritage with her mothers Indian spiced lamb kebab rolls with onion, mint, chilli and coriande

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the Canada-US border lies a vast lake that hides a dark history. For Native Americans its an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade plunges into the heart of a waterfall in the Pacific Northwest on a mission to catch the oldest river monster of his career

programme.title: Peter Andres 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter Andres 60 Minute Makeover is back and bigger than ever on Quest Red. Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home mkeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes. In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick tackles a tidy burglar who insists on clearing up the houses he robs, and investigates a suspicious church organist. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at the impact of summer in the northern hemisphere and winter in the south, revealing that wildlife faces feast, famine, fire and torrential rain in different regions around the world. On Marion Island life has frozen to a standstill, with king penguin chicks facing starvation as their parents left months ago, while in northern Australia its the dry season, and hooded parrots raise their chicks in termite mounds

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads for Derbyshires Peak District to help the owner of a 15th-century inn deal with an array of problems. The two-star venue has 10 en-suite bedrooms, a bar and a 50-seat restaurant, but struggles to attract customers at weekends. With a wage bill of £10,000 a month, the business is estimated to have made a loss of £600,000 over the years, and the expert hotelier sets out to improve the dated decor and turn around its fortunes

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain needs to face up to what his future might hold, while Joe feels unappreciated and Kim is surprised when she pays a visit to Moira

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary intervenes to reconcile Todd and Theo, Maggie orders Lauren to show Ollie what hes missing, Alya receives a concerning message, and Leanne overhears Megan flirting on the phone

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: When Matron is attacked by a thief, everyone is surprised to discover who was responsible - but there appears to be more to the case than meets the eye. Goodwin is upset when it becomes clear he has acquired a bad reputation around the hospital and the staff worry about the threat of a tuberculosis epidemic. Medical drama, starring Wendy Craig, Paul Fox, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The detective is paired with DC Webster, an officer who is struggling to fit in with life in Denton after being seconded from Scotland, with the duo investigating a robbery at a local casino and a hit-and-run incident involving Roger Massie, the son of a local politician, and soon finds that both matters are linked. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A CND rally at the Early Warning Installation creates chaos in Aidensfield, causing Nick to fall out with Kate and an MP to be blackmailed. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: The Body in the Lake
programme.desc: Tim is disgusted with Dani and wants her gone. At the school, a suspended Dani gathers her artwork as Sienna tries to offer some comfort, and they discuss their shared experience of foster care. Bel asks a nervous Sienna to speak at the vigil for Zac that night. Tim is confronted by Dani about the texts, but he shrugs off her questions

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todds friends rally round to support him, Adam and Jodies feuding turns dirty, Leanne consults Daniel about Sam, and Ollie chastises Maggie for meddling in his affairs

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rhona expresses her gratitude, but is spotted by Lydia, while Laurel confides in her friend Nicola

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Hermans behaviour becomes increasingly erratic and he is sent on compulsory leave. Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: Michael Palin in Nigeria
programme.desc: The actor and comedians latest adventure takes him on a 1300-mile journey across the West African country. He visits Makoko, which is often characterised as the biggest slum in Africa, and drops in at the luxury bars of Lekki, where music business insider Idara Idemeko explains the ambition of a generation now living the high life. He also travels to the coastal town of Badagry, which was once a slave port

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Robert Rinder examines the case of Claire Throssell. After years in a controlling marriage, she managed to leave, taking her two young sons with her. Life would have been perfect, if it wasnt for Claires ex-husband Darren Sykes still having access to their children. During one fatal unsupervised visit, Claires life would be destroyed

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: NYC
programme.desc: In Brooklyn, Ryan hustles to land his first new development under Serhant, and Fredrik celebrates his decision to go bi-coastal and announces new plans to expand his empire. Dreaming of a bigger team, KJ makes her own announcement, Steve hits the ice to lock down his 57th Street rental, and Tyler celebrates an emotional closing. After a wild year, the agents toast to new beginnings and the return to a new normal.
